turns.content: What brings you in today?
My stomach's been hurting off and on for a few weeks. I just don't feel like eating much anymore.
When did this start?
Maybe three or four weeks ago. It just kind of crept up on me.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
It's right here, sort of in the middle of my belly. Feels like a dull ache mostly, but sometimes it gets sharper.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No fever. No chills either. Just the stomach pain.
What medications are you currently taking?
Just ibuprofen sometimes, when it hurts too bad. My back gets sore from standing at work all day too.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, nothing like that. Just my mom had high blood pressure, but I don't have it.
Are you allergic to any medications?
Not that I know of. I had a rash from a bandage once, but nothing from pills.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
I take the ibuprofen when it gets bad. Sometimes it helps a little, sometimes not. Mostly I just wait for it to go away.
Does anything make it better or worse?
Eating sometimes makes it worse, especially if it's a heavy meal. I've just been having soup or crackers. Standing all day at the register doesn't help either.
How has this been affecting your daily life?
I've been skipping meals and just picking at food. It's hard to focus at work when my stomach's acting up. I feel tired all the time now.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No smoking. I'll have wine maybe once a week, just a glass or two with dinner. Not much.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not that I know of. My mom had some stomach troubles, but she never really said what it was.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. I usually feel fine, just normal aches from work. This is different for me.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yeah, I think so. Like I said, my stomach just hurts and I don't feel like eating. That's about all I know.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically maybe a 4. It's not the worst, but it's there most days. Emotionally, I guess a 3 — I'm just tired of feeling this way and hoping it'll go away.
Do you have any questions for me?
I guess I just want to know what you think it is. And if there's something I can take to make it stop, that'd be good.
What do you think might be going on?
I got no idea. That's why I came to you. I just want to feel normal again.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
No, I think that's it. Just the stomach hurting and not wanting to eat anything. That's all I've got.
